div.p: To the whole body of Christ's faithful who shall view or hear the present writing, Nicholas Weaver, burgess of Berwick, sends greeting in the Lord. Know, all of you, that since I conveyed to the abbot and convent of Melrose half of my land at Briggate in pure and perpetual alms, and sold the other half in return for one hundred merks of legal sterling, just as the seal which they have from me concerning the same land testifies; the said abbot and convent, for the increase of the aforementioned money, have given and granted to me [and] Hilda, my wife, or to others of ours who live for a longer time, for the whole of our life, 10 bolls of marketable wheat and one chalder of marketable barley and a half mark of silver each year at the feast of Saint Michael, to be received at Berwick. Wherefore, I, by this my charter, bind myself and all my goods, moveable and immovable, to the said abbot and convent, so that if by chance they are put to trouble on account of a defect of warranty relating to the aforementioned lands, or they lose them in part or in whole, they shall immediately cease from the payment of this increase. And whatever I or Hilda my wife shall have received from this increase, we shall fully and wholly restore it to the same with the hundred merks above said, and with all costs or losses they may have incurred, without dispute. As witness of this matter I, for myself and my heirs, have caused my seal to be put to this writing. As witnesses, the lord William the Earl's son, the lord Nicholas his son, John of Letham, and others. Enacted in the year of grace 1251, on the eleventh of the kalends of March [19 February 1250 n.s.]
